title: Human Activity Recognition System
description: Master's thesis project developing a hybrid deep learning model for real-time human activity recognition. Combines multiple CNN architectures for robust classification across diverse activity types.
institution: Southwest University of Science and Technology

title: YOLOv7-Based Surgical Tool Detection
description: Bachelor's thesis implementing YOLO variants for real-time detection and localization of surgical instruments in laparoscopic videos. Optimized for edge device deployment in operating room environments.
institution: Southwest University of Science and Technology

title: Autonomous Robot Motion Planning
description: Developed intelligent motion planning using deep reinforcement learning and computer vision. Integrated path planning with real-world obstacle avoidance for autonomous navigation systems.

title: Person Re-Identification System
description: Multi-task deep learning model with attribute-specific heads for cross-camera person re-identification. Achieved state-of-the-art accuracy on standard benchmark datasets.

title: End-to-End Speech Recognition Pipeline
description: Implemented complete speech recognition system combining signal processing with deep neural networks. Supports real-time audio transcription with noise robustness.

title: Medical Image Analysis Framework
description: Comprehensive framework for medical image processing and analysis using advanced computer vision techniques. Designed for disease detection and biomedical research applications.

title: Edge AI Deployment System
description: Optimized deep learning model deployment on IoT devices and edge computing platforms. Implements quantization and pruning techniques for real-time inference.

title: Hybrid Intelligence Research
description: Research on combining symbolic AI with deep learning for hybrid systems. Explores cognitive alignment between neural and symbolic approaches.

title: AI Ethics & Fairness Framework
description: Developing frameworks for ethical AI systems with focus on bias detection and fairness metrics. Addresses structural bias in machine learning and epistemic injustice issues.

title: Brain-Computer Interface Research
description: Research on neural signal processing for human-computer interaction. Applications include rehabilitation systems and advanced assistive technologies.
